prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='CWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.89) and high seasonality variance (0.69), with THETA leading trend tracking and CWT_catboost dominating seasonal capture. Early horizons favor THETA while late horizons prefer FT_svr, indicating horizon specialization. Given the strong disagreement signals and tiered model performance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "THETA",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "FT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_svr", "THETA", "ETS", "CWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "DWT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.8937,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.89) and high seasonality variance (0.69), with THETA leading trend tracking and CWT_catboost dominating seasonal capture. Early horizons favor THETA while late horizons prefer FT_svr, indicating horizon specialization. Given the strong disagreement signals and tiered model performance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all use past windows only or are baselines. Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). Redundancy check: no ≥4 candidates of same type. Universe POCID leaderboard shows ade_dynamic_error variants with POCID 78.79 but current best has POCID 72.73 - not a clear replacement. No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician:
{
  "add_names": ["ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim0.6"],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low, so conservative approach. Current set has strong RMSE coverage (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 is #1 in universe). Adding ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim0.6 provides POCID diversity (universe shows ade variants achieve 78.79 POCID vs current dba_combination's 75.76). relative_spread_mean=0.098 < 0.25, so disagreement is not high enough to favor DBA heavily. n_unique_winners=8 >= 3 supports per-horizon selection methods already present.",
  "changes": ["added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim0.6 for POCID diversity"],
  "when_good": "current set has topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 as #1 in universe RMSE, good coverage with weighted and selection methods"
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.89) and high seasonality variance (0.69), with THETA leading trend tracking and CWT_catboost dominating seasonal capture. Early horizons favor THETA while late horizons prefer FT_svr, indicating horizon specialization. Given the strong disagreement signals and tiered model performance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon specialization requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread between FT_svr (890.53) and DWT_svr (1851.68) shows significant model dispersion favoring top-k over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.69). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early/late horizon specialists separately.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all use past windows only or are baselines. Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). Redundancy check: no ≥4 candidates of same type. Universe POCID leaderboard shows ade_dynamic_error variants with POCID 78.79 but current best has POCID 72.73 - not a clear replacement. No changes needed.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low, so conservative approach. Current set has strong RMSE coverage (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 is #1 in universe). Adding ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim0.6 provides POCID diversity (universe shows ade variants achieve 78.79 POCID vs current dba_combination's 75.76). relative_spread_mean=0.098 < 0.25, so disagreement is not high enough to favor DBA heavily. n_unique_winners=8 >= 3 supports per-horizon selection methods already present.
statistician.when_good: current set has topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 as #1 in universe RMSE, good coverage with weighted and selection methods
